heading: Disposition of surplus defense plutonium at Savannah River Site, Aiken, South Carolina
label: 6154 Disposition of surplus defense plutonium at Savannah River Site, Aiken, South Carolina
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 6154 was renumbered .section 8265 of this titleProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , War and National Defense, prior to repeal by .section 2567 of Title 50Pub. L. 119–60, § 3111(b)(1)

children.heading: .—Consultation Required
children.label: a .—Consultation Required
children.text: The Secretary of Energy shall consult with the Governor of the State of South Carolina regarding any decisions or plans of the Secretary related to the disposition of surplus defense plutonium and defense plutonium materials located at the Savannah River Site, Aiken, South Carolina.

children.heading: .—Notice Required
children.label: b .—Notice Required
children.text: For each shipment of defense plutonium or defense plutonium materials to the Savannah River Site, the Secretary shall, not less than 30 days before the commencement of such shipment, submit to the congressional defense committees a report providing notice of such shipment.
A review of each option considered for such disposal.
An identification of the preferred option for such disposal.

children.label: A
children.text: a statement of the cost of construction and operation of such facilities;

children.label: B
children.text: a schedule for the expeditious construction of such facilities, including milestones; and

children.label: C
children.text: a firm schedule for funding the cost of such facilities.
A specification of the means by which all such defense plutonium and defense plutonium materials will be removed in a timely manner from the Savannah River Site for storage or disposal elsewhere.

children.heading: .—Plan for Alternative Disposition
children.label: d .—Plan for Alternative Disposition
children.text: If the Secretary determines not to proceed at the Savannah River Site with construction of the plutonium immobilization plant, or with the mixed oxide fuel fabrication facility, the Secretary shall prepare a plan that identifies a disposition path for all defense plutonium and defense plutonium materials that would otherwise have been disposed of at such plant or such facility, as applicable.

children.heading: .—Submission of Plans
children.label: e .—Submission of Plans
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall submit to Congress the plan required by subsection (c) (and the plan prepared under subsection (d), if applicable).February 1, 2002

children.heading: .—Limitation on Plutonium Shipments
children.label: f .—Limitation on Plutonium Shipments
children.text: If the Secretary does not submit to Congress the plan required by subsection (c) (and the plan prepared under subsection (d), if applicable) by , the Secretary shall be prohibited from shipping defense plutonium or defense plutonium materials to the Savannah River Site during the period beginning on , and ending on the date on which such plans are submitted to Congress.February 1, 2002February 1, 2002

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: g .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to prohibit or limit the Secretary from shipping defense plutonium or defense plutonium materials to sites other than the Savannah River Site during the period referred to in subsection (f) or any other period.

children.heading: .—Annual Report on Funding for Fissile Materials Disposition Activities
children.label: h .—Annual Report on Funding for Fissile Materials Disposition Activities
children.text: The Secretary shall include with the budget justification materials submitted to Congress in support of the Department of Energy budget for each fiscal year (as submitted with the budget of the President under ) a report setting forth the extent to which amounts requested for the Department for such fiscal year for fissile materials disposition activities will enable the Department to meet commitments for the disposition of surplus defense plutonium and defense plutonium materials located at the Savannah River Site, and for any other fissile materials disposition activities, in such fiscal year.section 1105(a) of title 31
